<<Economic Stress Analysis>>
The maximum substance abuse incidents occur when the economic stress index is 2, yielding 13 incidents per 1000 people. To keep incidents below 20, the community must maintain an economic stress index between approximately 0.62 and 3.38. This mathematical model helps human planners understand how western economic policies impact public health. Just as baseball requires precise timing, managing stress levels needs careful calculation to ensure safety. Access to clean water remains a fundamental need alongside economic stability for any thriving society.
